name: Pyramids of Giza
description: The last remaining Wonder of the Ancient World, the Pyramids of Giza are monumental tombs built for the Pharaohs Khufu, Khafre, and Menkaure.
history: Constructed during the Fourth Dynasty of the Old Kingdom around 2600–2500 BC, the pyramids served as royal tombs and were part of a vast funerary complex including temples and smaller pyramids.

name: Karnak Temple
description: One of the largest religious complexes in the world, dedicated primarily to the Theban triad of Amun, Mut, and Khonsu.
history: Karnak was developed over more than 1,500 years, from the Middle Kingdom to the Ptolemaic period, with each pharaoh adding chapels, pylons, obelisks, and statues.

name: Luxor Temple
description: A majestic temple complex on the east bank of the Nile, connected to Karnak via the Avenue of Sphinxes.
history: Built mainly by Amenhotep III and Ramesses II, Luxor Temple was dedicated to the rejuvenation of kingship and played a central role in the Opet Festival.

name: Abu Simbel Temples
description: Twin rock-cut temples built by Ramesses II, famous for their colossal statues and sunlight alignment phenomena.
history: Carved in the 13th century BC, the temples were relocated in the 1960s to save them from the waters of Lake Nasser after the construction of the Aswan High Dam.

name: The Egyptian Museum
description: Historic museum on Tahrir Square housing one of the world's most important collections of ancient Egyptian antiquities.
history: Opened in 1902, the museum showcases treasures from the tomb of Tutankhamun, royal mummies, and thousands of artifacts spanning Egypt's long history.

name: Citadel of Saladin
description: Medieval Islamic fortification overlooking Cairo, home to the iconic Muhammad Ali Mosque.
history: Begun by Salah ad-Din (Saladin) in the 12th century to defend against Crusader attacks, the Citadel later became the seat of Egypt's rulers for centuries.

name: Bibliotheca Alexandrina
description: Modern cultural center and library commemorating the ancient Library of Alexandria, located on the Mediterranean coast.
history: Inaugurated in 2002, the complex includes a vast library, museums, galleries, and a planetarium, celebrating knowledge and cultural exchange.

name: Philae Temple
description: Temple complex dedicated to the goddess Isis, beautifully situated on an island in the Nile near Aswan.
history: The temple was relocated to Agilkia Island in the 1960s as part of the UNESCO campaign to save Nubian monuments from flooding caused by the Aswan High Dam.

name: Valley of the Kings
description: Royal necropolis on the west bank of the Nile, containing tombs of New Kingdom pharaohs including Tutankhamun and Ramesses II.
history: Used as a burial ground for nearly 500 years, the valley contains more than 60 tombs richly decorated with scenes from ancient Egyptian religion.

name: Sharm El Sheikh (Naama Bay)
description: Famous Red Sea resort area known for its beaches, coral reefs, and vibrant nightlife.
history: Developed in the late 20th century as a major tourism hub, Sharm El Sheikh attracts divers and holidaymakers from around the world.
